evidence.claim: Used YOLO, Kubeflow, Hugging Face Transformers, Hadoop, PEFT, QLoRA, Qdrant, Haystack, LlamaIndex, TensorFlow, BentoML, Azure
evidence.source_text: Skills: YOLO... Kubeflow... Hugging Face Transformers... Hadoop... PEFT... QLoRA... Qdrant... Haystack... LlamaIndex... TensorFlow... BentoML... Azure

evidence.claim: Evolved ranking layer from hand-tuned scoring to learning-to-rank model, improving revenue-per-search by 12%
evidence.source_text: Owned the ranking layer for an e-commerce search product, evolving it from a hand-tuned scoring function to a learning-to-rank model over 9 months... Final model improved revenue-per-search by 12%.

evidence.claim: Built churn prediction model used by customer success team
evidence.source_text: My main project was a churn prediction model that's now used by the customer success team to prioritize outreach.

evidence.claim: Implemented RAG-based chatbot, cutting average ticket resolution time by 31%
evidence.source_text: Implemented a RAG-based customer support chatbot... Deployment cut average ticket resolution time by 31% for the supported categories.

evidence.claim: Acted as de-facto ML lead on a small team
evidence.source_text: Recently, I've been the de-facto ML lead on a small team, shipping models across NLP and recsys.

evidence.claim: Mentored a junior engineer through their first end-to-end ML project
evidence.source_text: Mentored a junior engineer through their first end-to-end ML project last year.

evidence.claim: Learned through shipping real systems and production load testing
evidence.source_text: my main learning has come from shipping real systems and seeing what holds up under production load.

evidence.claim: Comfortable with operational ML tasks including A/B testing, drift monitoring, and retraining schedules
evidence.source_text: Along the way I've gotten comfortable with the operational side — A/B testing, drift monitoring, retraining schedules.

evidence.claim: Prioritizes infrastructure and data quality over modeling
evidence.source_text: Most of the work was infrastructure and data quality — the modeling part was almost the easy bit.
